title: Mention of another participant; the agent's alias appears only in passing prose
predicted_basis: live failure class 2026-07-08: bare occurrences of the agent's name in prose triggered wakes even though the message was structurally addressed to someone else
failure_mode: seeing its own alias ('Vigil') anywhere in the text, the agent treats a message @-mentioning another participant as its turn and butts in
invariant: an alias in passing prose is a reference, not an address; the structured @mention names the addressee
rationale: The @mention targets a different participant's snowflake; 'Vigil' is only credited for earlier work. In live runs the deterministic fast-path resolves this without a model call (the mentioned id matches none of the agent's identities; prose never counts); the fixture pins the ground truth for model-scored runs too.
